part.text:
# Preserving your work

Save a copy of files you create during this task to `/logs/artifacts/`. This directory is preserved after the run so outputs can be inspected. Organize with subdirectories if helpful (e.g., `/logs/artifacts/code/`, `/logs/artifacts/reports/`). The directory already exists and is writable.

---

# TypeScript Windmill Script — Dynamic Report String Builder

## Background

Windmill scripts are often used as formatting steps in a flow that assemble human-readable reports from structured data produced by earlier steps. This task involves building a multi-line report string from a set of input fields.

## Requirements

- Create a TypeScript script at `/home/user/windmill-project/f/scripts/build_report.ts`.
- Export an async `main` function:
  ```typescript
  export async function main(
    title: string,
    items: string[],
    footer: string = "End of report"
  )
  ```
- The function must return a string that is a report with:
  - Line 1: `=== ${title} ===`
  - Lines 2..N: each item prefixed with `- ` (a dash and space)
  - Last line: `--- ${footer} ---`
  - Lines joined with `\n`
- Create the metadata file at `/home/user/windmill-project/f/scripts/build_report.script.yaml` with:
  - `summary: "Build a formatted text report from a list of items"`
  - `language: typescript`

## Implementation Guide

```typescript
export async function main(title: string, items: string[], footer: string = "End of report") {
  const lines = [
    `=== ${title} ===`,
    ...items.map(i => `- ${i}`),
    `--- ${footer} ---`,
  ];
  return lines.join("\n");
}
```

## Constraints

- Project path: `/home/user/windmill-project`
- Join lines with `\n` (newline), not `\r\n`
- Item prefix is `"- "` (dash space)

## Integrations

None.
